summary: PR 2266 restored/added lbArchive_LoadSections in src/melee/lb/lbarchive.c. The new helper iterates over varargs as symbol-output pointer/name pairs until a NULL symbol pointer sentinel, clears each output pointer, loads it through HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress, and reports missing symbols with OSReport.

key_files.role: Restores/adds lbArchive_LoadSections helper near lbArchive_InitializeDAT and lbArchive_LoadArchive_inline.

what_changed.text: Added void lbArchive_LoadSections(HSD_Archive* archive, void** symbol, ...) to src/melee/lb/lbarchive.c.
The helper uses va_start(symbols, symbol), loops while symbol != NULL, reads a const char* symbol_name from the varargs, and advances to the next void** symbol with va_arg(symbols, void**).
For each symbol output pointer, the function sets *symbol to NULL, then assigns HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress(archive, symbol_name).
If lookup returns NULL, the helper reports "Cannot find symbol %s.\n" via OSReport using the symbol name.

smart_moves.text: The function explicitly initializes each output slot to NULL before attempting the archive lookup, preserving a safe failure state if HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress cannot resolve the symbol.
The varargs API uses a NULL symbol pointer sentinel to terminate the section-loading list, keeping a compact call pattern for loading multiple archive public symbols.
Missing archive public symbols are reported immediately with the requested symbol name, which makes archive content issues searchable at runtime.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When restoring archive-loading helpers, preserve observable varargs contract details such as pointer/name argument ordering, NULL sentinel termination, and failure reporting strings; these details are part of the helper's caller-facing behavior.

naming_conventions.text: The restored helper follows the existing lbArchive_ function prefix used by nearby archive helpers.
Local names reflect the varargs roles directly: symbol for the current output pointer, symbol_name for the archive public symbol name, and symbols for the va_list.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: No explicit matching tactic such as pragma changes, PAD_STACK, register steering, inline assembly, or data-section manipulation is evidenced in this PR slice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Preserve lbArchive_LoadSections varargs contract when touching archive section loading
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2266 restores lbArchive_LoadSections as a varargs helper that takes output pointer/name pairs, terminates on a NULL output pointer, clears each output slot, resolves the public address through HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress, and OSReports missing symbols. Future decomp work around this helper should avoid changing this caller-facing contract without caller evidence.
